big_picture: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed the escalating Middle East tensions, condemning Iran's strikes in a call with the UAE President, while also chairing a late-night CCS meeting to discuss the situation. The Union Minister for Home & Cooperation, Amit Shah, led a consultative committee meeting on cybercrime, highlighting the growing threats and challenges in this area. Meanwhile, India is set to strengthen its ties with Canada, with former Bank of England Governor Mark Carney meeting with PM Modi to mend rifts and boost trade. The Opposition Congress has criticized PM Modi's handling of the Iran crisis, with the situation in West Asia being deemed a matter of grave concern for India.

The airspace closure has also left 109 persons from Karnataka stranded in West Asia.

articles.title: Treasury bonds, dollar remain reliable safe havens in crisis: Peter Cardillo
articles.summary: Global markets opened cautiously as geopolitical tensions escalated, boosting demand for safe-haven assets like the U.S. dollar and Treasury bonds. Oil prices experienced a 'shock trade' due to uncertainty surrounding the Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy chokepoint. India and China face significant challenges, potentially impacting their trade and economic stability.

India and China face significant challenges, potentially impacting their trade and economic stability.

articles.title: MHA alerts states as Middle East tensions soar; internet curbs, security tightened in Kashmir
articles.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs has alerted states to potential tensions due to the Iran conflict. Authorities are monitoring individuals and groups to prevent unrest in Jammu and Kashmir. This is a precautionary measure following protests over the killing of Iran's supreme leader.

articles.title: Defence stocks surge on Middle East tensions! HAL, BEL, Paras Defence rise up to 13% even as stock market crashes
articles.summary: Defence stocks in India have surged due to rising tensions in the Middle East. This has led to increased expectations of export opportunities for Indian defence companies. Companies like HAL, BEL, and Paras Defence have seen significant gains in their stock prices.
articles.raw_summary: ​​The escalation has raised expectations of increased export opportunities and strengthened investor sentiment toward the sector. Paras Defence led the gains, climbing 13.5%. Meanwhile, HAL, BEL and Bharat Dynamics advanced by up to 3.5% on the BSE.

articles.title: Middle East tensions: Gold prices jump 2% on Iran conflict; India braces for diamond supply crunch
articles.summary: Gold prices surged as major strikes on Iran, reportedly killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, intensified geopolitical tensions and fears of economic instability. The attacks triggered further retaliatory actions, creating a volatile environment that benefits gold as a safe-haven asset. Analysts predict continued price increases, with major banks forecasting gold to reach $6,000-$6,300 per ounce.

articles.title: Centre's SIM binding directive on app-based messaging platforms 'unconstitutional': Tech industry body BIF
articles.summary: The Indian government has issued a directive requiring app-based messaging platforms like WhatsApp, Signal, and Telegram to be linked to a user's active SIM card. The Strait of Hormuz, vital for energy imports, remains a key chokepoint.

articles.title: Base year revision lifts GDP estimates, captures structural changes in Indian economy: Experts

Analysts say the update enhances credibility, aligns with global standards, and improves policy assessment, with positive implications for equities, fiscal stability and investment outlook.

Fiscal deficit and debt ratios for upcoming years will likely be revised upwards.
